subject: [PATCH 3/5] iova: limit_pfn-aware augmentation for log(n) 32-bit alloc

2026-05-13.patch_summary: This patch improves the performance of DMA allocation in Linux by introducing a new augmented field that bounds the search by 32-bit-clamped gap size. This allows for O(log n) allocation time even when dealing with mixed-DMA-mask domains, which previously degraded to O(n). The change is achieved by precomputing and storing the clamped gap size on each iova node, enabling pure function callbacks without domain context. The update also maintains both the original and new augmented fields, allowing for seamless transition between 32-bit and 64-bit allocations.
